title: verbascum lagurus subsp. stefanovii
intro: Verbascum lagurus subsp. stefanovii, also known as the Bulgarian mullein, is a subspecies of the mullein plant, native to Bulgaria, characterized by its tall spikes of white or yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Verbascum lagurus subsp. stefanovii include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or ointment, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
